relationships: Vehicle belongs to a Transporter (vehicle.transporter → Transporter.id) and has a VehicleType (vehicle.vehicle_type → VehicleType.id)

Arrival references one Vehicle (arrival.vehicle) and one Driver (arrival.driver); its gate_ins array embeds EmptyVehicleGateIn entries by id/entry_no, and gate_outs array embeds SalesDispatch entries by id/entry_no

EmptyVehicleGateIn references a Vehicle, a Driver, and a parent VehicleEntry (vehicle_entry int FK); reason field is an enum (BST, DISPATCH, REPAIR_MOVEMENT, JOB_WORK, OTHER) defined by EmptyVehicleInReason
EmptyVehicleGateOut references a Vehicle, a Driver, and a VehicleEntry (vehicle_entry int FK); it closes out an EmptyVehicleGateIn session
VehicleEntry (DOCKV-prefixed) is the docking-level vehicle session created when a vehicle enters for loading; it is referenced by EmptyVehicleGateIn, EmptyVehicleGateOut, and SalesDispatch

SalesDispatch (DOCK-prefixed) is the primary gate-out record for a dispatch; it references Vehicle, Transporter, Driver, Arrival, DispatchPlan, and VehicleEntry; its item_summary field carries SAP FG item codes (e.g. FG0000143) bridging to product master
SalesDispatch embeds a documents array of SalesDispatchDocument-shaped objects each carrying sap_doc_entry/sap_doc_num, customer_code, customer_name, warehouses, and item_summary with FG SKU codes

DispatchPlan is the pre-dispatch booking record; it links vehicle_id, transporter_id, driver_id, and linked_vehicle_entry_id; its gate_out_id FK closes the loop back to a SalesDispatch once dispatched; carries sap_invoice_doc_entry/sap_invoice_doc_num as the originating SAP invoice
SalesDispatchDocument (pending-documents endpoint) represents an unassigned SAP invoice awaiting dispatch assignment; it embeds a DispatchPlan (plan.id) when a plan has been created for it
BstOutSapTransfer contains SAP inventory transfer documents (doc_entry/doc_num) eligible to be linked to a BstOut gate entry; from_warehouse and to_warehouse are SAP warehouse codes

PartialScanRequest and ScanSkipRequest both reference a SalesDispatch (sales_dispatch FK) and record supervisor approvals (requested_by, reviewed_by User FKs) for barcode-scan exceptions during outbound loading
SalesDispatchLock is a singleton per company that gates whether new SalesDispatch records can be created; changed_by FK references a User

ItemGroup is a SAP item-group reference (item_group_code bridges to SAP item master) used in non-moving raw-material reporting; item_group_code values include RAW MATERIAL (106), FINISHED (102), PACKAGING MATERIAL (105)
notes: CLI: jivo-factory-pp-cli (~/go/bin), authenticated. Scope: Company-Code JIVO_MART (company id=2). All endpoints under /api/v1. Entry-number prefixes observed: ARV- (Arrival), EVGI- (EmptyVehicleGateIn), EVGO- (EmptyVehicleGateOut), DOCKV- (VehicleEntry docking session), DOCK- (SalesDispatch). SAP FG item codes in item_summary follow pattern FGxxxxxxx (e.g. FG0000143). Six endpoints returned empty results (bst-ins, bst-outs, bst-returns, job-work, job-work-sap-grpos, job-work-sap-production-orders, rejected-qc-returns, sales-dispatch-pending-bookings, empty-vehicle-ins-eligible, bst-ins-eligible-outs, bst-returns-eligible-outs) — entity shapes must be inferred from endpoint names and context; no field-level data was available. The vehicles-names, transporters-names, and drivers-names subcommands are lightweight read views (id + identifier only) of Vehicle, Transporter, and Driver respectively — not separate entities. dispatch-plans returns a pipeline-board structure (columns + cards) rather than a flat list; plan_id is the DispatchPlan key. The non-moving-rm endpoint is a single flat endpoint (not a subcommand group) returning a wrapped object with a data array and meta object.

entities.name: ArrivalSlip
entities.record_count: 589+ (IDs seen up to 589; API returned 8 in current pending view)

entities.name: Inspection
entities.record_count: 8 active (same IDs as arrival-slips; workflow_status NOT_STARTED for all current records; inspection_id is null until QC started)

entities.name: GRPOEntry
entities.record_count: 5 (all in QC_PENDING phase; grpo pending returned empty; grpo history returned empty)

relationships: ArrivalSlip references VehicleEntry (vehicle_entry_id / entry_no) — each gate arrival generates one ArrivalSlip per PO line item received
ArrivalSlip references POItemReceipt (po_item_receipt) and POReceipt (po_receipt_id) — slip ties to the purchase order receipt in the PO domain
ArrivalSlip references User (submitted_by) — the gate/QC staff who submitted the slip
Inspection references ArrivalSlip (arrival_slip_id) one-to-one — the inspection record is the QC workflow record for each arrival slip; inspection_id is null until QC is formally started
Inspection references VehicleEntry (rejected_qc_return_entry_id) — when material fails QC and is returned to vendor, the return gate entry is linked here
Inspection references MaterialType (material_type_name) — classifies what kind of raw/packing material is being inspected
GRPOEntry references VehicleEntry (vehicle_entry_id / entry_no) — GRPO posting is triggered after QC approval of the gate entry
GRPOEntry references POReceipt (po_receipts[].po_receipt_id) — each GRPO entry groups one or more PO receipts
GRPOEntry references PurchaseOrder (po_numbers[]) — the SAP PO numbers being goods-receipted
GRPOEntry references Vendor (suppliers[].supplier_code) — the vendor whose material is being receipted
GRPOEntry references POItemReceipt (po_receipts[].items[].po_item_receipt_id) — individual line-item receipts within a PO
GRPOServiceEntry references DispatchPlan (dispatch_plan_id) — service GRPO is for outbound dispatch service invoices, not inbound material
GRPOServiceEntry references VehicleEntry (linked_vehicle_entry_id / linked_vehicle_entry_no) — the outbound gate exit event linked to the dispatch
ArrivalSlip.po_item_code and Inspection.po_item_code bridge to product/item master (SAP PM-prefix codes e.g. PM0000087 = packing material items)
GRPOEntry.po_receipts[].items[].item_code carries the same SAP item codes as ArrivalSlip.po_item_code — confirming items flow from ArrivalSlip through Inspection to GRPOEntry posting
notes: Filtered inspection views (inspections-actionable, inspections-awaiting-chemist, inspections-awaiting-qam, inspections-completed, inspections-draft, inspections-pending, inspections-rejected, inspections-return-to-vendor) are all the same Inspection entity filtered by workflow_status/qc_stage — not separate entities. inspections-counts and production-qc-counts return single aggregate objects (not lists). grpo summary and grpo service-options are aggregate/reference objects, not per-record lists; service-options contains branches[] and tax_codes[] reference data. The GRPO material flow is: VehicleEntry (gate) → ArrivalSlip (per PO line) → Inspection (QC decision) → GRPOEntry (SAP posting). GRPOServiceEntry is a separate outbound flow for service invoice posting against dispatch plans. All visible ArrivalSlip and GRPOEntry records are in QC_PENDING / workflow_status NOT_STARTED state, so inspection_id fields are null; no completed inspections are currently in the dataset.

entities.name: DispatchSession
entities.record_count: ~188 active/in-progress; ~118 completed; 0 closed (also accessible via barcode dispatch-sessions-completed and barcode dispatch-sessions-closed)

entities.name: DispatchSessionLine
entities.record_count: embedded in DispatchSession.lines array; not independently paginated

entities.name: ScannedUnit
entities.record_count: embedded in DispatchSession.scanned_units array; not independently paginated

entities.name: IntercompanyTransfer
entities.record_count: ~1,021 (exposed via dashboard endpoint; lines embed BarcodeBox references with item_code)

entities.name: IntercompanyTransferLine
entities.record_count: embedded in IntercompanyTransfer.lines array; ~27,875 total barcodes across all transfers

entities.name: ItemOITM
entities.record_count: ~500+ (SAP OITM item master; FG-prefixed finished-goods codes)

relationships: BarcodeBox belongs to a Pallet (BarcodeBox.pallet → Pallet.id) and optionally to a DispatchSession (BarcodeBox.dispatch_session → DispatchSession.id)
Pallet optionally belongs to a DispatchSession (Pallet.dispatch_session → DispatchSession.id) and aggregates many BarcodeBoxes
DispatchSession contains one or more DispatchSessionLines (embedded lines array), each identifying a material_code (SAP item) and warehouse_code
DispatchSession contains ScannedUnits (embedded scanned_units array) that reference both BarcodeBox.id and Pallet.id, bridging scans back to ScanHistory via scan_log
RejectedScan references a DispatchSession via bill_number and records the barcode string that was rejected during scanning
DispatchReport, DispatchReportBox, and DispatchReportPallet are flat projection views of DispatchSession, BarcodeBox, and Pallet respectively — keyed by session_id, box_id, and pallet_id
LooseItem links a source BarcodeBox (and optionally source Pallet) to a repacked-into BarcodeBox, forming a repack traceability chain for partial/damaged cartons
PrintHistory records every label print event for both BarcodeBox and Pallet entities (discriminated by label_type field)
ScanHistory records every scan attempt (success or failure) against BarcodeBox or Pallet entities, contextualised to a DispatchSession via context_ref_id

IntercompanyTransfer carries lines (IntercompanyTransferLine) each of which references a BarcodeBox.id and carries an item_code, enabling cross-company traceability between JIVO_OIL, JIVO_MART, and JIVO_BEVERAGES
ItemOITM is the SAP product master (OITM table); item_code fields across BarcodeBox, Pallet, DispatchSessionLine, LooseItem, and IntercompanyTransferLine all link to ItemOITM.item_code
DispatchSession references the SAP AR Invoice and Delivery document via bill_number / delivery_number and references Customer master via customer_code
notes: All 15 subcommands under `barcode` were sampled. Dispatch sessions are exposed as three filtered list endpoints (active/closed/completed) representing the same DispatchSession entity at different lifecycle stages — closed returned empty at time of sampling. DispatchSettings is a singleton config (results is a plain object, not an array). IntercompanyTransfer and IntercompanyTransferLine are exposed via the intercompany-dashboard endpoint which also returns today's summary stats and per-route aggregates. The `barcode items-oitm` endpoint mirrors the SAP OITM item master and serves as the product/SKU bridge across the entire domain. BarcodeBox barcodes follow the pattern BOX-YYYYMMDD-{line}-{seq}; Pallet barcodes follow PLT-YYYYMMDD-{line}-{seq}; IntercompanyTransfer numbers follow ICBT-{timestamp}.

entities.name: BiltyGrpoOptions
entities.record_count: 8 branches, 24 tax_codes, multiple GL accounts (reference/config data, not paginated records)

entities.name: DispatchPlanPipelineCard
entities.record_count: 19 (all in DISPATCHED stage; 10 pipeline stages: BOOKED, EMPTY_IN, READY_TO_DOCK, DOCKED, PHOTO_ATTACHED, READY_FOR_GATEPASS, GATEPASS_PRINTED, PRINT_COMMITTED, DISPATCHED, REJECTED)

relationships: DispatchPlan.linked_vehicle_entry_id references VehicleEntry (EVGI-series entry numbers like EVGI-20260627-0014); the vehicle that physically carried the goods
DispatchPlan.sap_invoice_doc_entry references a SAP Invoice (doc_entry is the SAP internal id; sap_invoice_doc_num is the human-readable invoice number like 706260628)
DispatchPlanPipelineCard.plan_id is the same identifier as DispatchPlan.dispatch_plan_id — the pipeline is a kanban view over the same DispatchPlan entity
DispatchPlanPipelineCard.vehicle_id references Vehicle master (also exposed via vehicle_no string)
DispatchPlanPipelineCard.gate_out_id references GateOutEntry (DOCK-series entry numbers like DOCK-20260629-0022); the docking/exit gate event
DispatchPlanPipelineCard.gate_out_vehicle_entry_id references VehicleEntry (the vehicle entry record that was used at gate-out)
DispatchPlanPipelineCard.empty_gate_in_entry_no is a string FK (EVGI-series) referencing the empty vehicle gate-in event prior to loading
DispatchPlan.transporter_name and transporter_gstin together identify a Transporter (no integer FK in this domain; GSTIN is the unique transporter key)
BiltyGrpoOptions.branches provide the Branch reference list used to assign source_state on a DispatchPlan
BiltyGrpoOptions.gl_accounts carry SAP account_codes (e.g. 1101001) used for GL posting when a bilty-GRPO is created against a DispatchPlan
TransporterInvoice (empty) is expected to reference DispatchPlan records via transporter_name/GSTIN once populated
notes: Pipeline stages observed on dispatch-plans (kanban): BOOKED → EMPTY_IN → READY_TO_DOCK → DOCKED → PHOTO_ATTACHED → READY_FOR_GATEPASS → GATEPASS_PRINTED → PRINT_COMMITTED → DISPATCHED → REJECTED. Only DISPATCHED had live records (19) at time of sampling. The bilty-grpo-options endpoint returns a composite object (not a paginated list) — its sub-collections (branches, tax_codes, gl_accounts) are lookup/reference tables for the bilty creation form, not independent vault notes. BiltyGrpoHistory and OpenBilty are structurally identical to DispatchPlan but filtered to different lifecycle states; they were empty at sampling time. TransporterInvoice is a distinct accounting entity (one invoice per transporter covering multiple dispatch trips) — empty at sampling time. SAP invoice linkage is the primary bridge to product/order data in other domains.

relationships: Vendor (vendor_code) is the supplying party on purchase orders and GRPO records; vendor_code appears as a FK in PO and goods-receipt documents surfaced via the grpo interface

Warehouse (warehouse_code) is the receiving or source stock location on PO lines, GRPO entries, and warehouse movement records; warehouse_code appears as a FK across grpo, warehouse, and dispatch interfaces

NonMovingRMItemGroup (item_group_code) classifies raw-material and other inventory items by material category; RM items in non-moving stock reports carry an item_group_code that links back to this lookup — item_group_code 106 (RAW MATERIAL) is the primary category for procurement-side non-moving stock
notes: The po group exposes only two reference/lookup tables (vendors, warehouses) — no open-PO list endpoint is surfaced in this CLI group; the full PO transaction records are likely accessible via the grpo interface. Vendor codes follow two prefix families: VENDA (external third-party suppliers, transport companies, raw material vendors) and ORGV (internal org imprest accounts for named employees, e.g. ORGV000034 = AJIT SINGH ECOM IMPREST). Warehouse codes use location-prefix notation (BH = Bhakharpur, DL = Delhi Mayapuri, PB = Punjab, KT = Karnataka, RJ = Rajasthan, UP = Uttar Pradesh, DP = Dropship). The non-moving-rm endpoint returns results nested as {data: [...], meta: {total_groups, fetched_at}} unlike the flat results array pattern used by po endpoints.

relationships: ProductionRun belongs to a Line (line_id) and is planned against a SAPOrder (sap_order_id)
Machine belongs to a Line and its maintenance status maps to MaintenanceAsset (shared entity in production-execution and maintenance domains)
WasteLog records reference a ProductionRun (run_id) and carry a material_code (SAP item code)
BreakdownCategory is referenced by WorkOrder (breakdown-type work orders) and by downtime analytics per machine
ChecklistTemplate belongs to a Machine; MachineChecklist is an execution instance of a ChecklistTemplate on a Machine
LineConfig is the production-line configuration record belonging to a Line
LineClearance is the clearance sign-off record for a Line before/after a ProductionRun

MaintenanceAsset references AssetCategory, AssetLocation, AssetDepartment, and optionally a Line (for production-line assets); hierarchy level (PLANT/AREA/LINE/MACHINE/COMPONENT/UTILITY) defines its position in the asset tree
AssetDocument (types: MANUAL, WARRANTY, AMC, SERVICE_REPORT, CALIBRATION, OTHER) and AssetPhoto both belong to a MaintenanceAsset

WorkOrder references a MaintenanceAsset (asset_id), optionally a Line (line_id) and a Department; work_types: COMPLAINT, BREAKDOWN, GENERAL, PREVENTIVE, INSPECTION, CALIBRATION, AMC_VENDOR, PROJECT; statuses: DRAFT, OPEN, ASSIGNED, IN_PROGRESS, WAITING_SPARE, WAITING_VENDOR, ON_HOLD, COMPLETED, APPROVED, CLOSED
WorkOrderPhoto belongs to a WorkOrder
PMPlan belongs to a MaintenanceAsset with a frequency (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY/HALF_YEARLY/YEARLY); PMExecution is one scheduled run of a PMPlan; PMChecklistItem is a step inside a PMPlan
Spare references SpareCategory; SpareMovement and SpareRequest both reference a Spare and a WorkOrder
VendorVisit references a MaintenanceAsset (asset under AMC or warranty); statuses: PLANNED, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED
WMSStockItem is keyed by (item_code, warehouse_code); foreign keys to Warehouse and WMSItemGroup
WMSStockMovement carries item_code (SAP SKU bridge) and warehouse_code; transaction_types include AR_INVOICE, TRANSFER, GRPO, Production Order (202)
WMSBatch links item_code (SAP SKU) to a Warehouse and holds batch_number, expiry_date, manufacturing_date

WMSTransfer is a multi-line SAP inventory transfer document; doc_entry (internal id) and doc_num (SAP number) identify the header; from_warehouse/to_warehouse are FKs to Warehouse; each line carries item_code
WMSSalesOrderLine is a backlog view of open SAP sales-order lines; keyed by doc_entry+line_num; warehouse_code FK to Warehouse; customer_code links to external customer master
WMSBillingItem is a GRPO billing-gap view per (item_code, warehouse_code); status: FULLY_BILLED, PARTIALLY_BILLED, UNBILLED; links to Warehouse
BOMRequest references item_code (finished-goods SAP SKU) for bill-of-materials withdrawal requests by production
FGReceipt references item_code (finished-goods SAP SKU) and warehouse_code (Warehouse) for inbound finished-goods receipts
item_code (format FG00000XX) is the cross-domain SAP/SKU bridge connecting WMS entities, SAPItem, SAPOrder, WasteLog, BOMRequest, and FGReceipt to the product master
notes: All production-execution list endpoints (lines, machines, runs, sap-items, sap-orders, waste, breakdown-categories, checklist-templates, line-configs, line-clearance, machine-checklists) returned empty arrays — the production MES module is configured but not yet populated with run data. Field definitions for these entities are inferred from analytics/dashboard/options endpoints that returned live aggregate data (reports-analytics, reports-analytics-monthly-summary, reports-analytics-oee, reports-analytics-downtime-pareto, reports-analytics-plan-vs-production). Similarly all maintenance CMMS list endpoints (assets, work-orders, spares, pm-plans, pm-executions, etc.) are empty; fields inferred from maintenance dashboard and maintenance options which returned live enum/count data. The maintenance options endpoint revealed all status enumerations: asset statuses (RUNNING, IDLE, BREAKDOWN, UNDER_PM, UNDER_REPAIR, RETIRED), work-order types (COMPLAINT, BREAKDOWN, GENERAL, PREVENTIVE, INSPECTION, CALIBRATION, AMC_VENDOR, PROJECT), work-order statuses (DRAFT, OPEN, ASSIGNED, IN_PROGRESS, WAITING_SPARE, WAITING_VENDOR, ON_HOLD, COMPLETED, APPROVED, CLOSED), PM frequencies (DAILY, WEEKLY, MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, HALF_YEARLY, YEARLY), PM execution statuses (PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, SKIPPED, OVERDUE), asset hierarchy levels (PLANT, AREA, LINE, MACHINE, COMPONENT, UTILITY). Warehouse WMS entities are fully live with real data (31 warehouses, 9 item groups, 953 stock positions, 300 batches, 14 active transfers, 86 open sales orders, 757 billing items). The CLI base is https://factory.jivo.in/api/v1 with Company-Code: JIVO_MART header. All item_code values follow the pattern FG0000XXX for finished goods.

entities.name: SalesPlanningRequirementRow
entities.record_count: empty (JIVO_MART not in supported_companies; data only in JIVO_OIL and JIVO_BEVERAGES)

relationships: Notification.reference_id is a polymorphic foreign key resolved by Notification.reference_type (observed values: 'person_entry'); links into gate/person entry entities in other domains

Notification.notification_type matches NotificationPreference.code — 23 preference codes seen (GATE_ENTRY_CREATED, QC_INSPECTION_SUBMITTED, GRPO_POSTED, PERSON_ENTRY_CREATED, PERSON_ENTRY_EXITED, etc.)
AuthenticatedUserProfile.companies[].company_id links to Company (company_id=1 JIVO_OIL, company_id=2 JIVO_MART, company_id=3 JIVO_BEVERAGES); User records carry no embedded company list

StockItem.item_code and InventoryAgeReportRow.item_code are SAP item codes (PM-prefix = packaging material, FG-prefix = finished good, RM-prefix expected for raw material) — bridge to product master in other domains
InventoryAgeReportRow.item_group references InventoryAgeFilterOptions item_group names (FINISHED, PACKAGING MATERIAL, RAW MATERIAL, etc.)
InventoryAgeReportRow.warehouse and StockItem.warehouse match warehouse codes in InventoryAgeFilterOptions.warehouses list (e.g. BH-FG, DL-GR, FBF-HR)

SalesPlanningRequirementRow.item_code is an SAP item code shared with StockItem/InventoryAgeReportRow — currently empty for JIVO_MART scope; analysis endpoint confirms it draws from SAP HANA procedure with forecast_id and open_po_qty fields
ProductionOrder.sku_code (FG-prefix) is an SAP finished-good item code shared with StockItem and InventoryAgeReportRow item_code namespace
ProductionOrderComponent.component_code (FG-prefix seen) is an SAP item code for BOM components; links to StockItem for live stock levels
ProductionOrderComponent belongs to ProductionOrder via prod_order_entry (the internal PK); prod_order_num is the SAP production order number
User records (accounts users) are the assignee/operator identity referenced by notification delivery and permissions lists in AuthenticatedUserProfile
notes: Scope is JIVO_MART company (company_id=2). All four resource groups covered: accounts (2 endpoints), notifications (3 endpoints), dashboards (6 endpoints), sap (3 endpoints). Key observations: (1) SalesPlanningRequirementRow is schema-complete but empty for JIVO_MART — the analysis endpoint explicitly lists supported_companies as JIVO_BEVERAGES and JIVO_OIL only. (2) ProcurementShortfall (sap plan-dashboard-procurement) returned empty results. (3) notifications/unread-count returns a scalar {unread_count: 12} — not a list entity, omitted as separate entity. (4) dashboards/sales-planning-requirement-status returns {latest: null, last_success: null} — scalar refresh timestamp, omitted as entity. (5) InventoryAgeFilterOptions is a reference/lookup container rather than a rows entity; item_group_code used as key_field for its item_groups sub-list. (6) SAP item code prefixes observed: PM = Packaging Material, FG = Finished Good; RM-prefix expected for Raw Material based on permissions listing barcode.add_loosestock etc.
